hypothesis: Training equivariant 3D molecular generative models with per-atom GFN2-xTB partial charges as an auxiliary supervision signal will produce generated conformers with statistically significantly lower mean GFN2-xTB relaxation energies (∆Erelax) and smaller average bond-length, bond-angle, and torsion deviations after GFN2-xTB optimization than identical models trained without charge supervision.
